text.title: Terraform IaC Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Terraform IaC methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each (environment, infra-layer) pair MUST be its own state workspace. prod-network, prod-data, prod-app, staging-network, etc. Cross-workspace dependencies via data sources or remote_state.
rule.rationale: Mixed-env state lets a dev apply touch prod; blast radius must be physically isolated.

rule.statement: Reusable patterns MUST come from a registry (Terraform Registry or private) with semver tags. Module callers reference version explicitly. Inline copy-paste forbidden.
rule.rationale: Modules in-place fork on first edit; registry + version enforces shared interface.

rule.statement: A module MUST NOT switch behaviour on env name (no if env == 'prod'). Pass behaviour as variables; let callers compose.
rule.rationale: Env conditionals couple modules to global state; variables keep modules pure.

rule.statement: Renames / re-parenting of resources MUST use moved blocks (TF >=1.1). state rm + import is reserved for genuine state corruption + requires named owner approval.
rule.rationale: moved blocks are reviewable + reversible; state surgery is destructive and bypasses review.

rule.statement: Cross-workspace references MUST go through data sources or terraform_remote_state. Hard-coded resource IDs (vpc-abc123) forbidden.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded IDs break on re-create; data sources self-heal.

rule.statement: Each workspace MUST document its blast radius: which resources, which env, which downstream services depend on it. Document lives in workspace README.
rule.rationale: Implicit blast radius surfaces only during incidents; documenting it lets reviewers reason about plans.
